diagnoses.description: ST elevation (STEMI) myocardial infarction involving right coronary artery

procedures.description: Dilation of coronary artery, one artery with drug-eluting intraluminal device, percutaneous approach

clinicalNotes.content: 67-year-old female presented with acute chest pain. EKG showed inferior STEMI. Emergent cath lab activation. RCA 100% occlusion found. Successful PCI with drug-eluting stent placement to RCA. Patient stabilized. PMH: Type 2 DM, HTN. Discharged to home in stable condition.
